title: Intense Silky Irish Lentils Stew
description: A bitter, silky one-pot Irish stew made with red lentils, fennel, and a namul‑blanch seasoning, served in a lacquerware bowl.
instructions: Heat a large pot over medium heat and sauté the fennel bulb until slightly caramelized, about 5 minutes.
Add the toasted fennel seed and dried namul seasoning, stirring for 30 seconds to release aromas.
Stir in the rinsed lentils, turmeric, and maple syrup, mixing until the lentils are coated.
Pour in the vegetable broth and water, bring the mixture to a gentle boil, then reduce to a simmer.
Cover the pot and let the stew cook for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the thawed spinach and continue simmering for another 10 minutes until the lentils are tender.
Stir in fresh lemon juice, then season with salt and pepper to taste.
Remove the pot from heat and let the stew rest for 5 minutes to thicken.
Ladle the stew into a lacquerware bowl, ensuring an even distribution of the silky broth.
Garnish with chopped parsley before serving.
